title: tillaea pedicellosa
intro: Tillaea pedicellosa, also known as Hawaiian Watercress or Hawaiian Tillaea, is a rare and endangered aquatic plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which are believed to include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Tillaea pedicellosa is traditionally prepared for herbal use by infusing its leaves in hot water, making a tea that is consumed for its medicinal and health benefits.
